description: §4.18.1 free-text mode: a plain-markup element whose body text BEGINS with `=` (`= hi`, `=`, a spreadsheet cell `=SUM(A1:A9)`) renders the literal `=` text. The tag-closing `>` terminates the opener; the leading `=` is display text — it is NOT a state-decl `=` nor a stray `>=` comparison operator. Regression guard for issue #28 (block-splitter tag-close leading-equals): the pre-fix S188 `>=` guard swallowed the `>` + leading `=`, corrupting the tag stack into an E-CTX-001 cascade on the outer tags.
rationale: §4.18.1: a plain-markup element (`<div>`, `<span>`, …) body is in FREE-TEXT mode — a bare (unquoted) run is display text. `= hi` / `=` / `=SUM(A1:A9)` are therefore literal text content, rendered verbatim. The opener's `>` closes the tag; the leading `=` is body text, so no E-CTX-001 (`tags don't match`) and no phantom `@wrap`/`@div` reactive-var (E-DG-002) may arise.
